training_backends.name: Musubi Tuner / Wan 2.1 T2V 1.3B
training_backends.description: The proven Wan video training lane: Windows app, WSL Ubuntu trainer, ROCm PyTorch, Musubi Tuner, and Wan 2.1 T2V 1.3B.
training_backends.best_for: Wan 2.1 video LoRAs, starting with short T2V motion/style experiments.

recommended_defaults.route_summary: This is slower than a big-VRAM setup, but it proved the 512px, 17-frame, rank 8 Wan video smoke test end to end.

notes: Wan 2.1 T2V 1.3B model bundle is present enough for this lane.
Selected DiT for this lane: models/wan/dependencies/dit/Wan2_1-T2V-1_3B_bf16.safetensors.
At least one Wan model bundle is present enough for the current Wan/Musubi lanes.
Place Wan GGUF inference experiments under models/wan/gguf/ and Musubi training dependency files under models/wan/dependencies/.

For the current Wan 1.3B and 14B training lanes, GGUF files do not satisfy the model-bundle requirement. Training looks for a task-matched DiT plus the shared T5 encoder and Wan VAE in models/wan/dependencies/.
Selected default Wan DiT for generated plans: models/wan/dependencies/dit/Wan2_1-T2V-1_3B_bf16.safetensors.
WSL Ubuntu, Musubi Tuner, and the Wan cache/train scripts were detected.
Generated scripts will set the ROCm/ROCDXG environment variables explicitly so they do not depend on an interactive terminal session.

training_backends.name: Musubi Tuner / Wan 2.1 T2V 1.3B / Image visual LoRA
training_backends.description: A sibling Wan/Musubi lane that trains still-image visual concepts into the same Wan 2.1 T2V 1.3B model family.
training_backends.best_for: Wan visual identity, character, object, and style LoRAs from still images before moving into video refinement.

recommended_defaults.route_summary: This image visual lane reuses the same cautious low-VRAM Musubi route as the first 1.3B video path.

training_backends.name: Musubi Tuner / Wan 2.1 T2V 14B
training_backends.description: An exploratory Wan video lane for the larger Wan 2.1 T2V 14B DiT inside the same Musubi Tuner workflow.
training_backends.best_for: Larger-capacity Wan 2.1 video LoRAs when you intentionally want the 14B path and have much stronger hardware than the first 1.3B smoke-test lane.

recommended_defaults.route_summary: This lane now follows the only 14B path that reached live training on the current WSL plus ROCm test rig: BF16-loaded weights, very low resolution, fewer frames, tiny rank, and maximum block swap. It is still experimental and may OOM before completing a step on 32GB-class systems.
recommended_defaults.hardware_note: Treat Wan 14B as a stronger-hardware experiment. On this project's current 8GB Radeon plus 32GB system-RAM box, even the squeezed route can exhaust WSL RAM plus swap after training starts.

notes: Wan 2.1 T2V 14B bundle is incomplete. This lane needs a matching DiT plus the shared T5 encoder and Wan VAE.
Wan 14B is bolted in as an exploratory lane. Expect materially higher VRAM and system-memory pressure than the proven 1.3B route.

On the current WSL + ROCm route, Wan 14B can be Linux-OOM-killed on CPU-side RAM/swap pressure before Windows Task Manager shows obvious GPU load. Treat WSL memory and swap as first-class requirements, not just VRAM.

The current experimental 14B handoff now avoids the FP8 weight-cast path on this chassis because the BF16-loaded route was the only local variant that reached live training before system memory became the next limit.
At least one Wan model bundle is present enough for the current Wan/Musubi lanes.
Place Wan GGUF inference experiments under models/wan/gguf/ and Musubi training dependency files under models/wan/dependencies/.

For the current Wan 1.3B and 14B training lanes, GGUF files do not satisfy the model-bundle requirement. Training looks for a task-matched DiT plus the shared T5 encoder and Wan VAE in models/wan/dependencies/.
Selected default Wan DiT for generated plans: models/wan/dependencies/dit/Wan2_1-T2V-1_3B_bf16.safetensors.
WSL Ubuntu, Musubi Tuner, and the Wan cache/train scripts were detected.
Generated scripts will set the ROCm/ROCDXG environment variables explicitly so they do not depend on an interactive terminal session.

training_backends.name: AI Toolkit / Wan 2.2 TI2V 5B
training_backends.description: A planned Wan 2.2 TI2V 5B lane for the Diffusers-style AI Toolkit workflow rather than the current Musubi safetensors route.
training_backends.best_for: A more achievable next-step Wan 2.2 lane once the local Diffusers bundle and AI Toolkit runtime are in place.

recommended_defaults.route_summary: This lane is meant to become the more achievable Wan 2.2 end-to-end path than the heavier 14B lanes, but the current Chatty-lora support is still scaffold-level until the local AI Toolkit workflow is proven.
recommended_defaults.hardware_note: Treat this as a promising but still unverified Wan 2.2 lane while the first real AI Toolkit handoff is being wired in.

notes: No local AI Toolkit folder detected yet under runtime/ai_toolkit or runtime/ai-toolkit.
Wan 2.2 TI2V 5B local model bundle looks complete enough for the scaffolded lane.
Selected Wan 2.2 TI2V 5B Diffusers bundle: models/wan/diffusers/Wan2.2-TI2V-5B-Diffusers.
This lane is scaffolded for AI Toolkit groundwork in Chatty-lora. The builder can now prepare a handoff folder, but the in-browser runner still only launches Musubi lanes.
Wan 2.2 5B is being treated as the more realistic next verified Wan 2.2 target than the heavier 14B routes, but the exact AI Toolkit launch recipe is still expected to evolve.

concept_blocks.concept_summary: the dog eating from a bowl

status_lines: Source registry and batched preview search are now wired.
Wan/Musubi plans now generate WSL handoff scripts and can run through the guided app runner.
starter_notes: Use the Materials page to select polite sources, search in 3-page batches, and preview material before curation.
Use the Builder page to pick a curated dataset, choose the Wan/Musubi backend, shape the starter settings, and save a reusable local training plan.
Saved Wan plans show an app runner plus manual fallback commands, so the first run stays visible and reversible.

Model families now live in dedicated buckets: Wan -> models/wan (Wan model family for training lanes, GGUF inference experiments, and future sibling variants.), Flux -> models/flux (Flux model family for future image-generation and LoRA workflows.), AI Assistant -> models/ai_assistant (Local helper models that support app features like assistant chat, source triage, and bug-testing workflows.), Audio -> models/audio (Future audio-model family bucket for transcription, tagging, and audio training support.).
This sister tool is standalone and does not share live code paths with Chatty-art.
